# harness_time_sleep (Resource)

Resource that introduces a time delay during create and/or destroy lifecycle phases.

Useful as a synchronization barrier — resources that `depends_on` this resource will wait until the sleep completes before proceeding. This avoids race conditions with eventually-consistent resources without resorting to `local-exec` provisioners.

Duration values use Go duration format: `ms` (milliseconds), `s` (seconds), `m` (minutes), `h` (hours). Examples: `"30s"`, `"5m"`, `"1h"`, `"500ms"`.

## Example Usage

### Create delay - pause 30s after delegate is created before pipeline runs

```terraform
resource "harness_platform_delegate_token" "mydelegate" {
  name       = "my-delegate"
  account_id = "YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID"
}

resource "harness_time_sleep" "wait_for_delegate" {
  create_duration = "30s"
  depends_on      = [harness_platform_delegate_token.mydelegate]
}

```

### Wait after destroying a delegate before tearing down dependents

```terraform
resource "harness_platform_delegate_token" "mydelegate" {
  name       = "my-delegate"
  account_id = "YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID"
}

resource "harness_time_sleep" "drain_delegate" {
  destroy_duration = "10s"
  depends_on       = [harness_platform_delegate_token.mydelegate]
}

```

### Both create and destroy delays

```terraform
resource "harness_time_sleep" "wait" {
  create_duration  = "30s"
  destroy_duration = "10s"
}
```

## Schema

### Optional

- `create_duration` (String) Duration to sleep when the resource is created. Accepts Go duration strings: `ms`, `s`, `m`, `h`. Example: `"30s"`, `"5m"`. At least one of `create_duration` or `destroy_duration` must be set.
- `destroy_duration` (String) Duration to sleep when the resource is destroyed. Accepts Go duration strings: `ms`, `s`, `m`, `h`. Example: `"30s"`, `"5m"`. At least one of `create_duration` or `destroy_duration` must be set.
- `triggers` (Map of String) Arbitrary map of string values. Any change to this map forces replacement of the resource, re-triggering the create and destroy delays.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) RFC3339 timestamp of when the resource was created. e.g. `2024-01-15T10:30:00.000000Z`.
